question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the server with requests that are never completed (e.g., by holding TCP connections open without sending FIN), preventing `res.on('finish', ...)` from ever firing, so `inFlightCounter.add(-1)` is never called, causing `inFlightCounter` to grow unboundedly and the in-flight tracking state to diverge from reality, ultimately exhausting event-loop capacity and partitioning the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent requests that each increment `inFlightCounter.add(1)` at line 169 but never reach `res.on('finish', ...)` due to abrupt client disconnects, causing the UpDownCounter `api_all_request_in_processing_total` to accumulate without bound and exhaust Node.js event-loop slots, effectively partitioning the mirror node from legitimate network peers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with a malformed `req.url` value (e.g., containing null bytes or invalid percent-encoding) that causes `new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost')` at line 153 to throw an unhandled exception, crashing the middleware chain and making the node unreachable to all network peers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a deeply nested or extremely long path containing many `:param` segments, triggering catastrophic backtracking in the regex `/:([^/]+)/g` at line 24 of `toOpenApiPath`, saturating the CPU and causing the node to stop responding to network traffic, effectively creating a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly hit the metrics endpoint (matching `metricsPath`) to trigger concurrent `exporter.collect()` Promise chains at line 161, and if `collect()` is not concurrency-safe, cause Promise queue saturation that blocks the Node.js event loop and partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely large `content-length` header value (e.g., `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`) that is parsed by `parseInt(req.headers['content-length'] ?? '0', 10)` at line 209 and recorded into `requestSizeHistogram`, causing histogram bucket computation to consume excessive CPU and degrade network responsiveness?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user generate high-cardinality label combinations by sending requests with many distinct HTTP methods, paths, and status codes, causing `requestTotalCounter`, `durationHistogram`, `requestSizeHistogram`, and `responseSizeHistogram` at lines 207-210 to accumulate millions of unique label sets, exhausting heap memory and triggering OOM that partitions the node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with attacker-controlled `req.path` values containing special regex characters or extremely long strings, causing the `path.replace(/:([^/]+)/g, '{$1}')` substitution at line 24 to consume unbounded CPU time and starve the event loop, preventing the node from processing legitimate network messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests where each response body is streamed in many small `res.write()` chunks, causing the overridden `res.write` at line 176 to call `getChunkSize()` thousands of times per response, accumulating CPU overhead that saturates the event loop and causes a de-facto network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getChunkSize()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger responses containing extremely large string chunks, causing `Buffer.byteLength(chunk)` at line 41 to be called on multi-megabyte strings repeatedly across thousands of concurrent responses, exhausting CPU and memory and causing the node to become unreachable on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `initMetrics()` is called once at line 147 and sets module-level shared state (`inFlightCounter`, `durationHistogram`, etc.) without any concurrency protection, so that a race condition between concurrent requests corrupts the metric state and causes the Prometheus exporter to enter an inconsistent state that blocks future `collect()` calls, partitioning the metrics pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `content-length` header set to a negative value or non-numeric string, causing `parseInt(req.headers['content-length'] ?? '0', 10) || 0` at line 209 to produce `NaN` or negative values that are recorded into `requestSizeHistogram`, corrupting histogram state and causing downstream metric consumers to misinterpret node health, masking a real network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely long `req.url` string that causes `new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost')` at line 153 to allocate a large URL object, and by repeating this at high frequency, exhaust the V8 heap and trigger garbage collection pauses long enough to cause TCP keepalive timeouts, partitioning the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: authenticate()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests to the metrics endpoint (`metricsPath`) with varying `Authorization` headers, causing `basicAuth(req)` and `tsscmp()` at lines 142-143 to be called at high frequency, consuming CPU proportional to credential length and degrading the node's ability to process legitimate network traffic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `res.write` and `res.end` are monkey-patched at lines 176-183 without checking if they have already been patched, so that if the same response object passes through the middleware twice (e.g., due to a routing misconfiguration), `responseSize` is double-counted and the patched functions are double-wrapped, causing stack overflows on deeply nested calls that crash the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the `res.on('finish', ...)` callback at line 185 to be registered multiple times on the same response object (e.g., via middleware re-entry), causing `inFlightCounter.add(-1)` to be called multiple times per request, driving the counter negative and corrupting the in-flight tracking invariant that operators rely on to detect network partition events?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where the `nodejs_process_cpu_usage_percentage` observable gauge callback at line 115 is invoked at extremely high frequency by the Prometheus scraper, causing `process.cpuUsage()` and `process.hrtime.bigint()` to be called in a tight loop, consuming CPU that would otherwise service network I/O and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly scrape the metrics endpoint to trigger the four `nodejs_process_memory_*` observable gauge callbacks at lines 131-133, each calling `process.memoryUsage()`, so that at high scrape frequency the cumulative overhead of these system calls saturates the event loop and prevents the node from maintaining network connections?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `req.method` value that is an extremely long string (e.g., thousands of characters), which is stored as the `method` label at line 192 and recorded into all per-route histograms and counters, causing label storage to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory, triggering OOM and a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where `res.locals[requestPathLabel]` is set to an attacker-controlled value (e.g., via a prior middleware that echoes user input into `res.locals`), causing `toOpenApiPath` to use that value as the metric label path, injecting arbitrary label values into Prometheus metrics and causing cardinality explosion that exhausts memory and partitions the node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of requests to non-existent routes, causing `req.route` to be `undefined` and `req.path` to be used directly in `toOpenApiPath` at line 18, resulting in unique per-path label values for every distinct URL path, creating unbounded label cardinality in `requestTotalCounter` and exhausting memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `exporter.collect()` at line 161 returns a Promise that is not wrapped in a try/catch, so that if the Prometheus exporter throws an unhandled Promise rejection during collection, it crashes the Node.js process (in versions where unhandled rejections are fatal), causing a complete network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `req.url` containing a Unicode surrogate pair or invalid UTF-8 sequence that causes `new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost')` at line 153 to throw a `TypeError`, and if this exception is not caught by the Express error handler, crash the worker process and partition the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `req.url` of exactly the metrics path but with varying trailing slash patterns (e.g., `//metrics/`, `/metrics//`) to bypass the `normalizedPath === metricsPath` check at line 156, causing the request to fall through to the instrumentation path and be double-counted in metrics, corrupting the accuracy of network health indicators?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: authenticate()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to the metrics endpoint with an `Authorization` header containing an extremely long username or password, causing `tsscmp(credentials.name, username)` at line 143 to process a very long string comparison, consuming CPU proportional to the longer string and enabling a CPU exhaustion attack that degrades network throughput?
